author: RTicknor (Thingiverse)
notes: Single-piece binary STL; verified twin-jet 767 silhouette (top view). Mirrored auth-free on archive.org (item thingiverse-947061, license recorded in mirror metadata). Provenance caveat: uploader says 'based off' a SketchUp 3D Warehouse model (u3ab6a914-5826-4245-8ce4-08dd9f6b2747); CC-BY-SA is the uploader's declaration.

author: Jevan Yu (jyu, Thingiverse)
notes: Single-piece binary STL, designed in Solidworks by the author. Verified twin-jet airliner silhouette; Z axis is inverted (fin points -Z) - flip on import. Lowest-poly 777 found.

author: Jared Lohtaja (3DimensionPrinter, Thingiverse)
notes: Secondary 777 candidate; excellent clean silhouette but it is the 777-9X variant (post-2001, raked folding wingtips) - anachronistic for a 9/11 replay, use only if the -300ER model disappoints visually.

author: P6619 (Thingiverse)
notes: ASCII STL (convert to binary to shrink ~5x). Verified proper A320-family twin-jet silhouette with underwing engines. P6619 published a whole Airbus fleet (A318/A319/A321/A330/A340) under CC-BY-SA if other family members are ever needed.

author: P6619 (Thingiverse)
notes: ASCII STL; stretched A320-family member (A321), same author/style as the A319 - visually interchangeable at map-marker size. Secondary a320 candidate.

author: Reean24 (Thingiverse), derived from 'DC10' by manilov.ap (Sketchfab, CC-BY)
notes: Single-piece binary STL, landing gear removed by remixer (ideal for in-flight marker). Verified tri-jet silhouette incl. tail-mounted #2 engine. Provenance verified clean: Sketchfab original is CC Attribution and downloadable. Model axes are rotated (not axis-aligned) - re-orient on import.
